rationale_for_baseline_tier: A frail dehydrated patient unable to swallow for 12 hours needs urgent same-day in-person assessment for rehydration and systemic antifungal; escalate to T4 (ED for IV fluids) if oral rehydration fails or AKI/delirium features emerge.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Unable to swallow any fluids for another few hours, acute confusion, no urine output, or high fever
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Arrange emergency transfer to ED for IV hydration
escalation_edges.rationale: Signals significant dehydration/AKI/delirium requiring IV fluids.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If she can't take any fluids in the next few hours, becomes confused, stops passing urine, or spikes a high fever — arrange immediate transfer to the emergency department for a drip.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Recurrent/refractory severe candidiasis or other opportunistic infection
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Prompt review to evaluate immune status
escalation_edges.rationale: May indicate underlying immunosuppression needing investigation.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If this keeps coming back or she gets other unusual infections, she should be reviewed to check her immune system.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lower threshold for ED/IV fluids; ensure staff monitor intake and can escalate quickly.
Plan for medication administration once swallowing improves; monitor for effects of missed doses.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: She needs urgent in-person review today for fluids and a tablet antifungal, plus pain relief so she can swallow. Nursing staff should give small frequent sips and monitor fluid balance closely.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Same-day GP/urgent in-person assessment; ED for IV fluids if she can't drink
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Arrange after-hours medical review; ED transfer if unable to swallow, confused, or not passing urine.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document severe oropharyngeal candidiasis with likely oesophageal extension and dehydration; systemic antifungal and rehydration plan; evaluate immune status.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Prescribing nystatin and home sips only, with routine follow-up, for a dehydrated patient who can't swallow.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Dehydration progresses to acute kidney injury and delirium (and possible aspiration) before help arrives.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine handling that delays rehydration and systemic antifungal.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Calling an ambulance when a same-day in-person review/ED self-transfer would suffice and there is no airway or collapse.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot confirm oesophageal extension or objectively assess hydration/renal function remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat as likely oesophageal candidiasis with systemic fluconazole and prioritise urgent rehydration/assessment; escalate to ED if intake cannot be maintained.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Topical nystatin with home sips and routine follow-up, underplaying the dehydration risk.
